title: Bridge Essay: Evolutionary aspects of self- and world consciousness in vertebrates.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Birth/Body** (Phase 1) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Birth/Body to mechanistic neuroscience through Evolutionary, aspects, of, self-, and.

**Evolutionary aspects of self- and world consciousness in vertebrates.** — Front Hum Neurosci. PMID: 25859205. DOI: 10.3389/fnhum.2015.00157. Although most aspects of world and self-consciousness are inherently subjective, neuroscience studies in humans and non-human animals provide correlational and causative indices of specific links between brain activity and representation of the self and the world. In this article we review neuroanatomic, neurophysiological and neuropsychological data supporting the hypothesis that different levels of self and world representation in vertebrates rely upon (i) a "basal" subcortical system that inc
